title: Austrostipa variabilis
taxonomy: Austrostipa variabilis is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is native to Australia and belongs to the genus Austrostipa, which is a group of Australian grasses. The genus Austrostipa is part of the subfamily Danthonioideae, within the family Poaceae. Austrostipa variabilis is further classified as a member of the tribe Danthonieae. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the Poaceae family.

intro: Austrostipa variabilis, commonly known as variable speargrass, is a perennial grass native to Australia. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation and respiratory problems, due to its reported health benefits. Research has identified the bioactive constituents of Austrostipa variabilis to include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Austrostipa variabilis include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and other respiratory conditions.
